dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9177 IH: Improving Mentorship in STEM Higher Education Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. McClellan (for herself, Mr. Tonko, Mrs. Foushee, Ms. Norton, and Ms. Ross) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology

form.official-title: To amend the Research and Development, Competition, and Innovation Act to direct the Director of the National Science Foundation to carry out a mentorship demonstration program, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Improving Mentorship in STEM Higher Education Act.

section.header: Mentorship demonstration program; National Science Foundation reporting

subsection.text: Section 10313(a) of the Research and Development, Competition, and Innovation Act (42 U.S.C. 18993) is amended by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.text: The Director may make awards, including through existing programs, on a competitive basis to eligible entities to carry out projects designed to improve mentorship practices and provide resources for faculty, graduate researchers, and postdoctoral researchers at the eligible entity, including with respect to—

clause.text: building and developing mentoring skills in such researchers and faculty;
promoting evidence-based and institutionalized mentorship at the eligible entity;
training such researchers and faculty in cultural competencies; and
funding outreach related to such projects.

subparagraph.text: An eligible entity desiring an award under this subsection shall submit to the Director an application at such time, in such form, and containing such information as the Director may require.
In making awards under this subsection, the Director shall give special consideration to eligible entities that are—

clause.text: rural-serving institutions of higher education (as such term is defined in section 861(b) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 (20 U.S.C. 1161q(b)));
consortia that include institutions described in clauses (i) through (v).

subparagraph.text: Not later than 5 years after the date of the enactment of this paragraph, the Director shall submit to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology and the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation and the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate a report that includes—

clause.text: an assessment, using qualitative or quantitative metrics as determined by the Director, of the effectiveness of the demonstration program carried out under this paragraph on increasing the quality of mentorship in STEM education; and
if such assessment determines that such program is effective, a plan to sustain and permanently implement such program.

subparagraph.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term eligible entity means—

clause.text: an institution of higher education (as such term is defined in section 101 of the Higher Education Act of 1965 (20 U.S.C. 1001));
a consortium of entities described in clauses (i) and (ii).

subparagraph.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this paragraph $5,000,000, for fiscal years 2027 through 2031.

subsection.text: Section 7009 of the America COMPETES Act (42 U.S.C. 1862o–1) is amended—

paragraph.text: by redesignating paragraphs (2) and (3) as paragraphs (3) and (4), respectively; and
by inserting after paragraph (1) the following:
institutional and agency reporting processes with respect to harassment, discrimination, and other forms of professional or scientific misconduct;
